summary: Iteration 22 — Hotfix v12.24 (GitHub push via httpx REST API, no subprocess/git binary). 14/14 new tests PASS + 26/26 regression (iter_20 pushlock + iter_21 gmail autosend) PASS after fixing ONE critical NameError. Validated: (a) ZERO subprocess imports and ZERO 'git' binary string references in user_workspace.py, (b) all 3 init scenarios — existing branch HEAD (200), new branch (404 → orphan commit), empty repo (409 → bootstrap via PUT /contents/README.md), (c) skip patterns (.git, __pycache__, node_modules, .pyc/.log/.db, files >1.5MB), (d) PAT format pre-check rejects 'invalid-format' before any HTTP call, github_pat_xxx + ghp_xxx pass format and surface 401 as 'Bad credentials' without Errno 2 crash, (e) iter_20 regression: export_locked still short-circuits BEFORE the REST-API block (zero GitHub calls when locked). Real GitHub push NOT executed (admin PAT invalid in preview DB per the request brief); all GitHub HTTP calls mocked via httpx.MockTransport to avoid rate-limit consumption.

critical.issue: NameError: name 'base64' is not defined at user_workspace.py:466. The 409 'Git Repository is empty' branch calls base64.b64encode(...) for the bootstrap README, but the module was only imported as `_b64` inside the inner `async with httpx.AsyncClient` block (line 501) — that import does not exist yet when the bootstrap code runs. Every push to a freshly-created empty repo would crash with NameError BEFORE any GitHub call. This is precisely the second production incident this hotfix was meant to address (HTTP 409). FIXED in this run by adding `import base64` at module top (line 21) and removing the redundant `import base64 as _b64` inside the function. Confirmed via test_empty_repo_409_bootstrap: was FAILED (NameError) → PASS after fix.

action_items: [DONE-BY-T1] Added `import base64` at module level in /app/backend/user_workspace.py (line 21) and removed inner `import base64 as _b64` shim. Main agent should review the diff and keep the change.

[INFO] Real end-to-end push to GitHub was NOT executed — the admin PAT in the preview DB is invalid per the brief. If a smoke test against a real empty repo is desired in production, run: POST /user/repos with auto_init=false → PUT /api/me/settings github_repo=<that repo> → POST /api/me/github/push. The mocked tests prove the code path is now correct.

[SUGGESTION] The do_push function is ~360 lines (user_workspace.py:252-613). Consider splitting into _collect_files, _bootstrap_empty_repo, _create_tree_and_commit, _update_ref helpers for maintainability. Not blocking.
critical_code_review_comments: user_workspace.py:466 — FIXED: base64.b64encode was called without the module being imported in scope. The latent inner `import base64 as _b64` at line 501 hid the bug because tests only exercising the 'existing' and 'new_branch' paths never reach line 466. This is a textbook case for top-level imports — never lazy-import a stdlib module that is referenced in an earlier branch of the same function.
user_workspace.py:413-415 — SKIP_DIRS and SKIP_EXTS match the brief exactly; .DS_Store is in BOTH dir skip and filename skip which is fine (defensive).
user_workspace.py:416 — MAX_FILE_BYTES = 1_500_000 matches the 1.5MB cap requirement.

user_workspace.py:265-288 — export_locked check sits BEFORE the REST API block; non-admin with balance<threshold returns early without any httpx call. Validated by test_non_admin_below_threshold_blocks_push (asserts len(captured_http_requests)==0).

user_workspace.py:611 — `auth_failed` flag string-matches 'rechazó tu token' on user_facing_error, but `user_facing_error` is set to None on success and there is no codepath that assigns this string. The auth_failed signal in the SUCCESS return is effectively always False. Real auth_failed propagation works via the explicit `auth_failed: True` keys in the early-return branches (lines 311, 318, 357, 484, 496, 516, 539, 563, 588). Not a bug per se but the line 611 expression is dead code — remove for clarity.

user_workspace.py:528-532 — Comment says 'Reemplazo total (no merge) usando un tree NUEVO desde cero' and intentionally omits base_tree from the POST body. This means every push uploads the full tree from scratch (no diffing). Acceptable for small user_apps workspaces (<100 files) but will get slower as the workspace grows. Document or consider passing base_tree for incremental pushes in the future.

user_workspace.py:47 — `if not token or len(token) < 10` happens BEFORE the prefix check, so very-short tokens that happen to start with ghp_ (e.g. 'ghp_short') get rejected by the length check first. Order is correct.

user_workspace.py:67-69 — `token_clean` is computed but then overwritten on line 69 via `token = token_clean` only inside the `if token_clean != token` branch; the prefix check on line 54 already used token_clean. Minor: cleaner to just use token = token_clean unconditionally up front. Not a bug.
updated_files: /app/backend/user_workspace.py (added module-level `import base64` at line 21; removed inner `import base64 as _b64` at former line 501; renamed `_b64.b64encode` to `base64.b64encode` at former line 504)
test_credentials: Admin melvinnavas79@gmail.com / Admin#2026 — not exercised live (all GitHub calls mocked). PAT for real push remains invalid in preview DB per brief.
seed_data_creation: All tests use tmp_path for the fake workspace; LLUVIA_HOME monkeypatched per-test. db is a MagicMock with AsyncMock methods. NO real Mongo writes, NO real GitHub API hits, NO data residue.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run with: `cd /app/backend && set -a && . ./.env && set +a && pytest tests/test_iteration_22_github_rest_push.py tests/test_iteration_20_pricing_pushlock.py tests/test_iteration_21_gmail_autosend.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/iter_N_results.xml`. The 8 tests in test_iteration_21 + 3 in test_iteration_20 require MONGO_URL exported (they hit real Mongo). The 14 tests in test_iteration_22 are pure mocks and need no env. If main agent ever does a real push smoke test, create the empty repo via API, then DELETE /repos/{repo} at the end as the brief requires.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: line 466 of user_workspace.py used `base64.b64encode(...)` but `base64` was only imported as `_b64` inside the inner `async with` block (line 501). The bootstrap code for the empty-repo (HTTP 409) path is reached BEFORE that inner import, so it raised NameError. The bug was invisible to any test that didn't hit the 409 path (which is why iteration_20/21 missed it, and why even the original v12.24 hotfix author missed it). REPRODUCTION: mock /git/refs/heads/{branch} to return 409 'Git Repository is empty.' and call do_push — instant NameError. MITIGATION: moved `import base64` to module top (line 21), removed redundant inner import, renamed `_b64.b64encode` to `base64.b64encode`. POST-FIX: 14/14 iter_22 tests pass, 26/26 regression tests pass. Production users who attempt to push to a freshly created empty repo will now successfully bootstrap via PUT /contents/README.md and then continue the normal blobs/tree/commit flow.
